interactions.summary: Jiang continues listing AI limits: open-ended meaning, real-world parameters, dirty data, and malicious human behavior. He says the solution is not smarter AI but remaking the world so humans obey AI.
The host asks whether dark fiction such as Hunger Games and Squid Game works like the same kind of preemptive cultural takeover.

Jiang calls recent civil-war and revolutionary films predictive programming that prepares America for racialized violence; the host adds that social media users feed a negative cycle, and Jiang pivots to a personal story about his viral Iran-war clip.

speaker_notes.note: Short host interjections keep the exchange moving; Jiang remains the main speaker.

claims.claim: Jiang says LLMs do not understand meaning; they summarize scraped language within bounded parameters and create the illusion of English competence.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's technical critique of AI in this interview.

claims.claim: Jiang argues self-driving cars expose AI's political limit: malicious humans can make robot systems fail, so the system's answer is to eliminate human driving and make the world safe for AI.
Jiang says films such as Civil War and One Battle After Another are predictive programming that prepare America for civil war and racialized violence.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's reading of contemporary film culture in this 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: The solution to AI's weakness is to redesign the world until human disobedience is removed from the dataset.
signature_moments.source_phrase: make the world safe for AI
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns AI limitation into a political program of obedience, a major interpretive hinge for the interview.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Media conditioning that Jiang says prepares people to accept or enact later political violence.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang reads recent films through a March 2026 civil-war-warning frame tied to his Iran-war visibility.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR title 'one battle after another' may refer to a film title; preserve as transcript phrase unless separately verified.
